item.title: Bob Dylan
item.description: Genres: folk rock, contemporary folk, singer-songwriter, blues rock, country rock Scene: Greenwich Village folk scene Era: 1961–present (primary period: 1962–1976) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Bob Dylan arrived in New York in January 1961 as a nineteen-year-old from Hibbing, Minnesota, carrying an ac...

item.title: Chicago blues
item.description: Blues → Electric blues → Chicago blues Period: Late 1940s–present (peak: 1948–mid-1960s) Location: Chicago, Illinois (South Side and West Side) Key influences: Delta blues, country blues, jump blues RYM Muddy Waters’s slide guitar cutting through the noise of a South Side club at two in the morning,...

item.title: Electric blues
item.description: Blues → Electric blues Period: 1938–present (peak: late 1940s–late 1960s) Location: Chicago, Memphis, Texas, Los Angeles Key influences: Delta blues, country blues, jump blues, gospel, jazz RYM A guitar string bent through a cheap amplifier in a loud club on Chicago’s South Side, the note hanging in...

item.title: Jump blues
item.description: Blues → Jump blues Period: Late 1930s–mid-1950s Location: Kansas City, Los Angeles, New York, Chicago Key influences: Swing, big band, boogie woogie, blues RYM A saxophone honks a two-bar riff, a piano hammers boogie woogie patterns underneath, and a singer half-shouts a lyric about drinking, dancin...

item.title: Rhythm & blues
item.description: R&B → Rhythm & blues Period: Late 1940s–mid-1960s Location: New York, New Orleans, Chicago, Los Angeles, Memphis Key influences: Jump blues, gospel, boogie woogie, swing RYM Ruth Brown’s voice on “Teardrops from My Eyes” (1950), caught between a shout and a sob over a shuffling beat and a sa...

item.title: Folk rock
item.description: Rock → Folk rock Period: 1965–early 1970s; continuing currents since Location: Los Angeles, New York (Greenwich Village), London Key influences: American folk revival, Rock & roll, British Invasion pop, Bob Dylan RYM A twelve-string Rickenbacker picks out a chiming arpeggio over a steady backbea...

item.title: Chess Records
item.description: Founded: 1950 (as Chess Records; predecessor Aristocrat Records from 1947) Location: Chicago, Illinois (2120 South Michigan Avenue, 1957–1967) Founders: Leonard Chess, Phil Chess Key years: 1950–1969 Subsidiaries: Checker Records (1952), Argo Records (1955; renamed Cadet, 1965) RYM The sound of 2120...

item.title: Sun Records
item.description: Founded: February 1, 1952 (predecessor Memphis Recording Service, January 3, 1950) Location: Memphis, Tennessee (706 Union Avenue) Founder: Sam Phillips Key years: 1952–1968 (sold to Shelby Singleton, July 1969) Subsidiaries: Flip Records (1955), Phillips International Records (October 1957) RYM The...

item.title: (1953) "Gee"
item.description: Artist: The Crows Released: June 1953 (b/w “I Love You So”) Label: Rama Records (Rama 5) Producer: George Goldner Studio: Beltone Studios, New York City Songwriters: William Davis, Viola Watkins Genre: Primary: Doo-wop RYM · Apple Music A jazz-toned electric guitar solo lands halfway through “Gee” w...

item.title: (1958) "Johnny B. Goode"
item.description: Artist: Chuck Berry Released: March 31, 1958 (b/w “Around and Around”) Label: Chess Records (Chess 1691) Producer: Leonard Chess, Phil Chess Songwriters: Chuck Berry Genre: Primary: Rock & roll Secondary: Rhythm & blues, Rockabilly RYM · Apple Music · Spotify A pickup lick in double-stops, f...
